song.title: So, What Do We Do?
song.albumTitle: Swipe Me to the Moon (A Gay Musical)
song.description: Full lyrics to 'So, What Do We Do?' from Swipe Me to the Moon (A Gay Musical) by Agust Islandia (Agust Smari Bjarkarson).

og.title: So, What Do We Do? Lyrics - Swipe Me to the Moon
og.description: Full lyrics to 'So, What Do We Do?' from Swipe Me to the Moon. 'Oh my god I just had sex with someone who smiled the whole time.'

twitter.title: So, What Do We Do? Lyrics - Swipe Me to the Moon
twitter.description: 'Oh my god I just had sex with someone who smiled the whole time.' Track 12 from Swipe Me to the Moon.

essay: So, What Do We Do? is the first serious decision-point in the relationship. It pivots from chemistry toward logistics, commitment, and the reality of shared direction.

What makes the song so powerful is its honesty — neither character knows the "rules," and in that uncertainty they discover something more valuable: someone who sees them completely and still wants more. The closing lines are among the most celebrated in the musical.
song.lyricsHtml:
Okay so uh do you want to go to my place or
Yes I mean yes definitely yes
Cool casual totally casual wow that's a nice lamp
We sat on the couch then both stood up
Then one of us tripped on a coffee cup

He touched my knee I kissed his chin
Then we both asked should we begin
I've never actually done this before
Wait same you too
Yeah I just watched a lot of videos and hoped for the best
God bless the internet

Do we undress at the same time
Should I take off your shirt or you take off mine
Do I ask what are you into
Or just say surprise me and go straight through
He kissed my neck and I giggled too loud
He touched my chest and I nearly bowed

We both tried to lead then bumped our heads
Then fell on the bed and whispered okay let's just figure it out
We don't need a manual we don't need to know
We'll go slow or fast or medium slow
Top bottom what's that even mean
Let's just make out and keep it clean

We're not breaking records or we're not in a race
But his lips fit mine and I love his face
This isn't a movie this isn't a test
It's two gay boys doing their best
He asked if I was okay like twice
He paused when I laughed and that felt nice

We didn't need music or candlelight
It was weird and warm and totally right
We figured out kisses we figured out hands
We got a few things tangled but hey that was planned
Oh my god I just had sex with someone who smiled the whole time
With someone who saw me and still wanted more
And holy shit I think I'm gonna text him again
